Foreign Professionals “Work Permit”｜Employment for Foreign Nationals in Taiwan

  • Post published:2024 年 8 月 30 日
  • Post category:Work Regulation

The types of work that foreign professionals can engage in while in Taiwan include the following seven categories: A. Specialized or technical work (subject to the Scoring Criteria), B. Managers of businesses invested in or established by overseas Chinese or foreigners with government approval, C. School teachers, D. Full-time foreign language teachers at cram schools, E. Sports coaches and athletes, F. Artistic and performance work, G. Contract performers, etc.

This document primarily introduces category A —— Specialized or technical work (subject to the Scoring Criteria), including qualifications, job categories, required documents for work permits (work certificates), and application methods.

  • General Criteria for Foreigners Working in Taiwan
  • The categories of Specialized or Technical Work
  • Application Process for Work Permit
  • Recommendations for Popular JobsHOT

General Criteria for Foreigners Working in Taiwan

Currently, foreigners engaging in specialized or technical work must meet one of the following qualifications:

  1. Obtain certification or professional qualifications according to the “Examination Act for Professional and Technical Personnel” (e.g., doctors, lawyers).
  2. Hold a master’s degree or higher from a relevant department of a domestic or foreign university or hold a bachelor’s degree from a relevant department with more than 2 years of related work experience.
  3. Have worked for a multinational enterprise for more than 1 year and have been assigned to work in Taiwan.
  4. Have more than 5 years of work experience without formal education credentials, with notable achievements or exceptional performance.

The average monthly salary for employed foreigners must reach at least NT$47,971, unless otherwise specified. The maximum duration for each employment permit application for foreign professionals is 3 years. Before the expiration of the employment period, an extension can be applied for, with no limit on the number of extensions. For those who qualify as specific foreign professionals, the maximum duration for each employment permit is 5 years.

The categories of Specialized or Technical Work

Applicants are encouraged to refer to the Ministry of Labor’s resources for detailed descriptions of eligible job types and further information. These regulations aim to provide a streamlined path for international students to transition from studying to working in Taiwan. The categories of Specialized or Technical Work, include:

(The links embedded pertain to the qualifications, required documents, and application forms for the respective categories of specialized or technical work)

Application Process for Work Permit

Once employment is confirmed, the employer is responsible for applying for the work permit. The entire process can be completed online.

Online Application

  • Employers should submit applications through the Foreign Professionals Work Permit Application Network.
  • The Ministry of Labor completes the review within 7 working days from the day after the case is received.
  • Consultation Phone Number: 02-8995-6000

Recommendations for Popular Jobs

  • 104 Job Bank
  • Jobs Hiring International Talents
  • Teaching positions
  • Translator positions
  • AI-related jobs
